promise: A human can install the repo, put real context on the canvas, inspect it, and hand the same state to Codex through MCP or a Codex export.
successDefinition: The local app starts from a GitHub clone without provider API keys.
The first viewport lets a human paste, drop, upload, type, or load the demo without reading docs first.
The captured material becomes typed source nodes with provenance, chunks, and inspector receipts.
A local action can create an inspectable output node from the source context.
Context can be exported as JSON, Markdown, agent context, or a Codex handoff prompt.
Codex can read and mutate the same local canvas through safe MCP tools.

phases.label: Install
phases.humanAction: Run the setup script from a GitHub clone.
phases.detail: Setup script, doctor, MCP build, smoke, seed, dry-run MCP config.

phases.label: Open
phases.humanAction: Start the app and land directly in the canvas workspace.
phases.detail: Start the app directly in the local canvas workspace.

phases.label: Capture
phases.humanAction: Paste or drop a YouTube/video link, image, URL, PDF, file, transcript, or note.
phases.detail: Paste, drop, upload, or type source context into the first viewport.
phases.appSurface: Add Anything composer, canvas toolbar, empty-canvas capture box, and drop surface

phases.label: Inspect
phases.humanAction: Select the created source/output and review provenance, chunks, citations, and body text.
phases.detail: Review source receipts, provenance, chunks, citations, and outputs.
phases.appSurface: Inspector source receipt, chunk preview, run log, and citation focus controls

phases.label: Handoff
phases.humanAction: Copy Context or Codex, or export JSON/Markdown for portable review.
phases.detail: Copy Context or Codex, or export JSON/Markdown from CLI or app.

phases.label: Codex
phases.humanAction: Install MCP config, restart Codex, and ask it to operate on the latest canvas.
phases.detail: Use MCP tools to read, ingest, run actions, and export the same canvas.

inputContracts.label: YouTube
inputContracts.output: source_youtube node with transcript/caption/manual fallback chunks
inputContracts.detail: Title/captions when available; manual transcript or notes remain first-class context.
inputContracts.codexUse: Codex reads chunk ids and source URL through MCP/context export.

inputContracts.label: Any video
inputContracts.detail: Preserves the URL as video evidence and uses pasted notes or transcripts for analysis.
inputContracts.codexUse: Codex receives the video reference plus attached notes/chunks without claiming provider transcription.

inputContracts.label: Image
inputContracts.output: source_image node with preview/provenance and notes/chunks
inputContracts.detail: Captures screenshots or image links with visual notes, OCR text, or observations.
inputContracts.codexUse: Codex receives image provenance and human/OCR notes; provider vision is optional future work.

inputContracts.label: Web
inputContracts.output: source_url artifact from bounded fetch or safe reference fallback
inputContracts.detail: Fetches readable text when safe; private/local targets are blocked by default.
inputContracts.codexUse: Codex receives fetched text chunks or the safe fallback reference.

inputContracts.label: PDF
inputContracts.output: source_pdf artifact from local capped extraction
inputContracts.detail: Extracts local PDF text into chunks with file provenance.
inputContracts.codexUse: Codex receives extracted chunks and file provenance through export/MCP.

inputContracts.label: Text
inputContracts.input: Text, Markdown, JSON, CSV, log, or transcript
inputContracts.detail: Turns raw pasted text or files into local chunks for actions and citations.
inputContracts.codexUse: Codex receives durable text chunks instead of a one-off chat paste.

inputContracts.label: Note
inputContracts.output: editable note node usable as selected context
inputContracts.detail: Keeps human synthesis editable, selectable, and exportable as agent context.
inputContracts.codexUse: Codex can read and build from the note while the human keeps editing control.

codexPrompt: Use starlight-agent-canvas as shared local context. Call get_latest_canvas, read the graph before writing, add durable evidence with ingest_anything when new context appears, run one useful action, then export_canvas with format "codex". Return node ids, artifact ids, chunk ids, and every node/action changed.
knownLimits: Non-YouTube provider transcript adapters are future work; v0.1 captures them as safe video references plus notes.
Image OCR and provider vision are future work; v0.1 captures image references/uploads plus human notes.
Hosted collaboration, auth, billing, marketplace, and provider-backed AI actions are v0.2+.
